key_insight: ToMap shows that in proof autoformalization, improving the decomposition step yields the biggest test-time gains, because high-quality atomic proof units unlock downstream formalization and proving.
engineering_relevance: The paper suggests a practical compute-allocation strategy for theorem proving systems: spend test-time budget on the bottleneck agent rather than applying broad, costly repair to every stage.
method: Test-time optimization focused on Decomposer refinement, not all agents
Prompt evolution over candidate decompositions using formal verification progress and semantic proof rubrics
evidence_signal: On ProofFlowBench, ToMap improves the prior best method by 19.0% on combined syntactic correctness and semantic faithfulness while reducing test-time cost.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliable verification signals and rubric quality, so performance may be sensitive to how well these objectives capture true proof correctness.
paper_title: Efficient Test-Time Optimization for Multi-Agent Proof Autoformalization
